content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 6, 1994


Thomas N. Townsend
1228 Dimondale Drive
Carson, CA 90746

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-94-148

Dear Mr. Townsend:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Since your request is
one for general information, we are considering it a request for
informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

Is a candidate who will not be listed on the June 7, 1994
ballot due to postponement of the election, required to file the
second pre-election campaign statement due May 26, 1994?

CONCLUSION

A candidate is not required to file the second pre-election
campaign statement in connection with the June 7, 1994 election if
the candidate's name will not appear on the ballot.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Mr. Townsend
Our File No. I-94-148
Page two

FACTS

     You filed as a candidate for the June 7, 1994 election for
Judge of the Compton Municipal Court. Due to the retirement of
Judge Hugo Hill, the June 7th primary election has been postponed
until November 8, 1994. The Los Angeles Registrar's Office has
advised me that your name will not appear on the ballot June 7,
1994. In addition, they advised me that you filed the first pre-
election campaign statement for that election. You indicated that
you are planning to file the semi-annual campaign statement which
is due July 31, 1994.

ANALYSIS

     The Act requires candidates being voted upon to file pre-
election campaign disclosure statements. However, because the
June election for the office of Compton Municipal Court Judge was
cancelled and your name will not appear on the ballot, the Act's
provisions pertaining to the second pre-election statement would
not apply. (Section 84200.5.)

     A municipal court judge candidate who has received
contributions or made expenditures must file a semi-annual
statement. Semi-annual statements are due no later than July 31
and January 31 for the period ending June 30 and January 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)

     Pursuant to Regulation 18116, when a deadline for a statement
or report falls on a Saturday, Sunday or official state holiday,
the filing deadline shall be extended to the next regular business
day. Please note that the July 31st semi-annual campaign filing
deadline falls on a Sunday. Therefore, the filing deadline is
extended to Monday, August 1, 1994.

     You have requested information pertaining to the reporting
requirements and dates for filing in connection with the November
8, 1994 election. The January/February/March issue of the FPPC
Bulletin contains the November election filing schedule. Enclosed

Mr. Townsend
Our File No. I-94-148
Page Three

please find the following materials:

-     Campaign Disclosure Information Manual A, Campaign
      Provisions of the Political Reform Act for
      Elected State and Local Officers (Including Judges)
      and their Controlled Campaign Committees
-     1994 Addendum to 1991 Information Manual A
-     Forms 501/502, Candidate Intention Statement and
      Campaign Bank Account Statement
-     Form 410, Statement of Organization
-     Form 490, Officeholder, Candidate, and Controlled
      Committee Campaign Statement--Long form
-     Form 470, Officeholder and Candidate Campaign
      Statement--Short Form and Form 470 Supplement
-     FPPC Bulletin

     If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Alice Hughes
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Assistance Division
